name: Accelerator
description: Issues related to the CEBAF or LERF accelerator systems, SRF cavities, RF zones, and linac components, including RF power supplies and magnet trim/box supplies along the beamline.

name: Injector / Source
description: Issues specific to the polarized electron source and injector that produce and initially accelerate the beam, including the photocathode, drive laser, and electron gun high-voltage systems, distinct from downstream linac/RF faults.

name: Beam Diagnostics
description: Faults in beam instrumentation used to measure and tune the beam, including beam position monitors (BPMs), beam current monitors (BCMs), harp/wire scanners, viewers, and Unser monitors.

name: Halls
description: Problems with experimental detectors, spectrometers, and hall-specific subsystems in Halls A, B, C, and D (e.g., CLAS12 in Hall B, GlueX in Hall D, HMS/SHMS in Hall C, BigBite/SuperBigBite in Hall A).

name: Cryogenics
description: Cryogenic system issues including the Central Helium Liquefier (CHL1/CHL2), End Station Refrigerator (ESR) serving Halls A/B/C, the Hall D Refrigerator (HDR), cryomodule cooling, and 2K/4K regulation.

name: Vacuum
description: Vacuum system problems including beamline ultra-high vacuum (UHV) leaks, insulating vacuum failures, ion pumps, turbo pumps, and gauge/valve malfunctions.

name: Magnets
description: Faults in the accelerator's magnet systems (dipoles, quadrupoles, correctors, steering magnets) or their power supplies, which can cause beam steering and transport problems or trip beam permit.

name: Targets
description: Issues involving experimental targets, including cryogenic targets (LH2/LD2), polarized targets, solid targets, or cell health and positioning.

name: EPICS
description: Experimental Physics and Industrial Control System (EPICS) issues, including IOC crashes, screen/GUI errors (MEDM/CS-Studio Phoebus), MYA archiver gaps, or control soft-IOC failures.

name: Power
description: Electrical power issues including voltage/current trips, main power supplies, circuit breakers, and building electrical infrastructure.

name: Safety
description: Safety system issues including the Personnel Safety System (PSS), Machine Protection System (MPS), and Oxygen Deficiency Hazard (ODH) monitoring/alarms.

name: Radiation Control (RadCon)
description: Radiation-control issues, including radiation area monitoring, dosimetry, beam-loss-related activation concerns, and radiological surveys — distinct from the PSS/MPS interlock hardware itself.

name: Network
description: Network connectivity, data acquisition (DAQ) networking, Computer Center / Scientific Computing resources, and electronic logbook (HALOG) access issues.

name: CODA
description: Software bugs, online computing tools, and JLab CODA (CEBAF Online Data Acquisition) system anomalies, including Readout Controllers (ROC), Event Builder (EB), Trigger Supervisor (TS), or readout list (ROL) problems.

name: Mechanical
description: Mechanical infrastructure, motorized positioning systems, valves, and Low Conductivity Water (LCW) cooling loops essential for magnets and RF components.

name: MCC
description: Machine Control Center (MCC) operations, shift handovers, operational logging, and coordination between operators, crew chiefs, and Hall leaders.
